events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: Baseplates & anchor bolts, Equipment & Machinery, in-progress. Observed field condition: insufficient anchor bolt projection. Reference: API RP 686.

API RP 686 covers machinery installation. Anchor bolt projection requirements - insufficient projection means the anchor bolts don't extend far enough above the baseplate/foundation to allow proper nut engagement, washer, and proper torquing. This is a non-compliant condition.

Sequence check: If anchor bolts are already installed (set in concrete/grouted) and have insufficient projection, this is a defect that must be corrected before proceeding with baseplate setting, grouting, and equipment alignment. The work cannot continue until anchor bolts are corrected (replaced, extended, etc.).

Decision: fail - non-compliant condition.
Risk: high - insufficient projection can prevent proper anchoring of equipment, leading to safety and reliability issues.
Work stage: in-progress.
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.
Percent complete: maybe 30-40% (anchor bolts installed but defective, baseplate work in progress).
Value: 0 (not asked for numeric reading).
Confidence: high, say 0.9.
Findings: insufficient anchor bolt projection - cannot achieve proper nut engagement/torque.
Actions: stop work, document nonconformance, engineer review for repair/replacement of anchor bolts, verify projection meets spec before proceeding.
Rationale: API RP 686 requires proper anchor bolt projection for nut engagement and proper torque. Insufficient projection is a nonconformance that must be resolved before baseplate setting/grouting/equipment alignment can proceed.
References: API RP 686.
